title: Japan's emperors conclude their state visit to Belgium with acts of friendship
summary: The Japanese sovereigns and King Felipe highlighted the ties of four generations during official dinners and speeches.

title: Japanese yen weakens towards 162 yen per dollar and official intervention grows
summary: The Japanese currency remains pressured by the dollar's strength amid expectations of a rate hike in the US, and authorities warn of decisive measures.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo scores two goals and sets the record for six consecutive World Cups scoring
summary: The 41-year-old Portuguese forward led his team's victory and became the first footballer to score in six different World Cups.

title: US Senate approves resolution limiting military action against Iran
summary: The upper house joins the House of Representatives to prevent an offensive without congressional authorization, amid tensions over Iran's nuclear program.

title: North Korea launches a new destroyer and announces the nuclearization of its navy
summary: Leader Kim Jong Un oversaw the ceremony of the vessel 'Choe Hyon' and declared that the fleet will be equipped with nuclear weapons.

title: US proposes national management of advanced artificial intelligence at the G7
summary: Washington urges allies to adopt a common regulatory framework for AI in light of cybersecurity risks and competition with China.

title: Two typhoons and heavy rain threaten Japan with landslides and floods
summary: Weather authorities issue warnings for torrential rain in the south of the country and the approach of typhoons Mekkhala and Higos.

title: Takaichi government presents its economic plan with emphasis on defense, AI, and budget
summary: The Japanese administration advances the outlines of its policy, which include a special budget for semiconductors and AI and the revision of the subsidiary capital law.

title: Japan's corporate services price index rises 3.3% in May
summary: The year-on-year increase remains stable compared to previous months, reflecting moderate inflation in the services sector.

title: Woman dies in parking lot and companion later dies in accident in Takasaki
summary: Police investigate the case that occurred in Gunma city, where the man who was with the victim later died in a road accident.

title: China detains Japanese employee for alleged rare earth smuggling
summary: Chinese authorities arrested a worker from a large electronics company for attempting to take regulated materials out of the country in machinery parts.

title: Osaka metropolis plan divides parties with alleged support from Prime Minister
summary: The initiative of the Ishin party to reform the prefecture generates controversy while local liberals reject Takaichi's stance.

title: Dow Jones replaces Verizon with Alphabet, Google's parent, in its index
summary: The change in the New York stock index reflects the growing weight of the technology sector.

title: Nissan's shareholders' meeting rejects re-election of an outside director
summary: Shareholders voted against the continuation of the director, questioning his independence due to his link with Mizuho.

title: Japanese Parliament pushes law against misinformation in election campaigns
summary: The regulation will prohibit the dissemination of false data about candidates and regulate the use of social networks during electoral periods.

title: Japan strengthens prevention after bear incidents in Akita and Utsunomiya
summary: Bear sightings and attacks increase, leading to capture drills and citizen complaints about wildlife management.

title: JR Yamanote line temporarily halted due to accident at Komagome station
summary: Service resumed after a personal incident that affected train circulation in Tokyo was handled.

title: Okinawa commemorates Memorial Day for all war dead
summary: The annual ceremony gathered authorities and family members to honor the victims of the conflict, with the attendance of Prime Minister Takaichi.

title: Japan's stock market suffers sharp fall due to collapse of global tech stocks
summary: The Nikkei recorded its fifth largest daily loss, dragged down by weakness in the semiconductor sector and doubts about the profitability of AI investment.

title: Japan strengthens military alliances with United Kingdom, Italy, Poland, and Australia
summary: Tokyo seeks to diversify its defense partners amid uncertainty with the United States and China's growing influence.
